title: Playout
description:
A playout schedule for a single time window.

Files should be named `{start}_{finish}.json` using compact ISO 8601 (no separators), e.g. `20260413T000000.000000000-0500_20260414T002131.620000000-0500.json`, so that the channel can locate the correct file for the current time.

version.description: URI identifying the schema version, e.g. "https://ersatztv.org/playout/version/0.0.1".

items.description: Ordered list of scheduled items for this window.

PlayoutItem.description:
A single scheduled item in the playout.

An item must supply media for its tracks. You can do this two ways, and they can be combined:

1. Set `source` to use one shared source for every track. Track details (which stream, etc.) are chosen naively, or may be refined via `tracks`.
2. Set `tracks` to specify each track (video, audio) individually. A track may provide its own `source`; if it doesn't, the item's top-level `source` is used.

At least one of `source` or `tracks` must be present, and every track that is selected must have an effective source (either its own or the item's).

id.description: Stable identifier for this item, unique within the playout.

source.description: The default source shared by any track that doesn't specify its own. Required unless every selected track in `tracks` provides its own `source`.

tracks.description: Per-track selection. Omit to let the server pick the first video and first audio track of the item's `source`.

watermark.description: Watermark (image/video overlay) to composite on top of the primary content for the duration of this item. Omit for no watermark.

PlayoutItemTracks.description: Per-track overrides for a playout item. Omit a field to use the server default for that track kind (first stream of that kind in the item's `source`, if any).

TrackSelection.description:
Selects a single track (video or audio).

- `source` absent: inherit the parent `PlayoutItem.source`.
- `source` present: use this source for this track, overriding the parent.
- `stream_index` absent: server picks the first stream of the track's kind in the effective source.
- `stream_index` present: use this specific stream within the effective source.

source.description: Source to pull this track from. If omitted, inherits from the parent `PlayoutItem.source`.

stream_index.description: Zero-based stream index within the effective source. If omitted, the server picks the first stream of this track's kind.

Watermark.description: An image or video overlay composited on top of the primary content. Sized and positioned relative to the primary content's frame.

source.description: The source providing the watermark media (typically an image, but any `PlayoutItemSource` is accepted).

stream_index.description: Zero-based stream index within the source. If omitted, the server picks the first video stream.

location.description: Anchor position within the primary content frame.

width_percent.description: Scale the watermark to this percent of the primary content width (0–100). Omit to use the watermark's actual size.

horizontal_margin_percent.description: Horizontal offset from the anchor `location`, as a percent of primary content width (0–100). Omit for 0.

vertical_margin_percent.description: Vertical offset from the anchor `location`, as a percent of primary content height (0–100). Omit for 0.

opacity_percent.description: Opacity as a percent (0–100). Omit for fully opaque (100).

within_source_content.description: When true, position margins are measured from the edges of the source content rather than the padded output frame, so letterbox/pillarbox bars push the watermark inward and keep it inside the visible content. When false, margins are relative to the full padded frame, so a 0% margin can land inside the bars. Has no effect when the primary content fills the output (crop/stretch). Omit for false.

timing.description: Visibility schedule for the watermark. Omit for an always-on watermark.

WatermarkTiming.description: Controls when the watermark is shown. Exactly one variant, distinguished by `timing_type`.

PeriodicTiming.description: Cyclical visibility: the watermark fades in, holds, and fades out once every `frequency_ms`. Cycle length is start-to-start, so `2 * fade_ms + hold_ms` must be ≤ `frequency_ms`, and `fade_ms` must be ≤ `hold_ms`.

clock.description: Reference clock used to position cycles.

frequency_ms.description: Period of the cycle, start-to-start, in milliseconds.

phase_offset_ms.description: Shift the cycle by this many milliseconds. With `clock: wall` and `frequency_ms: 300000`, an offset of 0 puts an appearance start at every wall-clock 5-minute boundary; 120000 shifts to :02, :07, :12, etc. Omit for 0.

disable_after_ms.description: Cap on the time, measured from the start of the playout item in milliseconds, during which new appearances are allowed to begin. An appearance already in progress at the cap is allowed to fade out cleanly. Omit for no cap.

fade_ms.description: Fade-in and fade-out duration in milliseconds (symmetric). Must be ≤ `hold_ms`. Omit for 1000; set to 0 for hard cuts.

hold_ms.description: Time held fully visible between fade-in and fade-out, in milliseconds.

PeriodicClock.description: Reference clock for periodic timing. `wall` aligns cycles to wall-clock time (so a viewer tuning in at any moment sees the same phase). `content` measures from the start of the containing playout item.

WatermarkLocation.description: Nine-position anchor within the primary content frame. Read like a 3×3 grid: rows top/center/bottom, columns left/center/right; the dead center is `center`.

PlayoutItemSource.description: A media source. Exactly one variant, distinguished by `source_type`.

LocalSource.description: A file on the local filesystem reachable by the server.

path.description: Absolute path to the media file.

in_point_ms.description: Optional start offset into the source, in milliseconds.

out_point_ms.description: Optional end offset into the source, in milliseconds.

LavfiSource.description: A synthetic source produced by an ffmpeg lavfi filter graph.

params.description: The lavfi filter graph parameters, passed verbatim to ffmpeg's `-f lavfi -i`.

HttpSource.description: A remote source fetched over HTTP(S).

in_point_ms.description: Optional start offset into the source, in milliseconds.

out_point_ms.description: Optional end offset into the source, in milliseconds.

reconnect.description: Enable reconnect on failure. Default: true.

reconnect_delay_max.description: Maximum reconnect delay in seconds. Maps to ffmpeg's `reconnect_delay_max`.
